questions.q: In which year was the Simon Commission sent to India?
questions.explanation: The Simon Commission arrived in India in 1928 to review constitutional reforms.

questions.q: Which famous slogan greeted the Simon Commission across India?
questions.explanation: The slogan 'Simon Go Back' was raised everywhere the commission travelled.

questions.q: Why was the Simon Commission so strongly opposed by Indians?
questions.explanation: The commission was entirely British, with no Indian members, which Indians found deeply insulting.

questions.q: After whom was the Simon Commission named?
questions.explanation: The commission was named after its chairman, Sir John Simon.

questions.q: What was the main task of the Simon Commission?
options: To review constitutional reforms in India
To investigate the Jallianwala Bagh massacre
questions.explanation: The commission was set up to examine the working of the 1919 reforms and recommend constitutional changes.

questions.q: Which kind of demonstrations commonly met the Simon Commission?
questions.explanation: Crowds waved black flags at the commission as a sign of protest.

questions.q: Which major Indian political party boycotted the Simon Commission?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress decided to boycott the commission entirely.

questions.q: Which national leader was fatally injured during a protest against the Simon Commission?
questions.explanation: Lala Lajpat Rai was injured in a lathi charge while protesting the commission in Lahore and died soon after.

questions.q: Of how many members was the Simon Commission composed?
questions.explanation: The commission consisted of seven British members, all from the British Parliament.

questions.q: The members of the Simon Commission belonged to which body?
questions.explanation: All seven members were British Members of Parliament, which is why the commission was called all-British.

questions.q: At which port city did the Simon Commission first land in February 1928?
questions.explanation: The commission landed at Bombay in February 1928, where it was met with a hartal and black-flag protests.

questions.q: In which city was Lala Lajpat Rai injured during the anti-Simon protest?
questions.explanation: Lala Lajpat Rai was struck during a lathi charge at Lahore when the commission visited.

questions.q: Which British police officer ordered the lathi charge that injured Lala Lajpat Rai?
questions.explanation: Superintendent James Scott ordered the lathi charge in Lahore in which Lajpat Rai was injured.

questions.q: The Simon Commission was set up under which earlier Act, which had promised a review after ten years?
questions.explanation: The Government of India Act 1919 provided for a review of the reforms after ten years, leading to the Simon Commission.

questions.q: Which future British Prime Minister was a member of the Simon Commission?
questions.explanation: Clement Attlee, later the Prime Minister who oversaw Indian independence, served as a member of the Simon Commission.

questions.q: The killing of which police officer by revolutionaries was carried out to avenge Lala Lajpat Rai's death?
questions.explanation: HSRA revolutionaries shot John Saunders in December 1928, intending to avenge Lajpat Rai but mistaking Saunders for Scott.

questions.q: What was the official name of the Simon Commission?
questions.explanation: Its official title was the Indian Statutory Commission, commonly known as the Simon Commission.

questions.q: Which Indian response to the Simon Commission attempted to draft a constitution for India in 1928?
questions.explanation: In response to the all-British commission, Indians produced the Nehru Report of 1928 as their own constitutional blueprint.

questions.q: Who chaired the committee that produced the Nehru Report of 1928?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru chaired the committee that drafted the Nehru Report in answer to the Simon Commission.

questions.q: In which year was Lala Lajpat Rai fatally injured during a Simon Commission protest?
questions.explanation: Lala Lajpat Rai was injured in the October 1928 protest against the commission and died in November 1928.

questions.q: The Simon Commission was appointed by the government of which British Secretary of State for India?
questions.explanation: The commission was appointed under Lord Birkenhead, then Secretary of State for India.

questions.q: Which Indian party largely supported or cooperated with the Simon Commission in the south?
questions.explanation: The Justice Party in Madras was among the few that cooperated with the commission rather than boycotting it.

questions.q: In which year did the Simon Commission publish its final report?
questions.explanation: The Simon Commission submitted its report in 1930, after touring India in 1928 and 1929.

questions.q: The Simon Commission's report fed into which subsequent series of London discussions on Indian reform?
questions.explanation: The commission's recommendations were taken up at the Round Table Conferences held in London from 1930 to 1932.

questions.q: The Government of India Act of which year was eventually shaped in part by the Simon Commission's recommendations?
questions.explanation: The Simon Commission's report contributed to the framing of the Government of India Act 1935.

questions.q: Which Labour MP and trade union leader, president of the South Wales Miners' Federation, sat on the Simon Commission?
questions.explanation: Vernon Hartshorn, a Welsh Labour MP and president of the South Wales Miners' Federation, was one of the seven members of the Simon Commission.

questions.q: On what date in October 1928 was Lala Lajpat Rai struck during the Lahore demonstration against the commission?
questions.explanation: Lajpat Rai was injured in the lathi charge on 30 October 1928 in Lahore and died on 17 November 1928.

questions.q: Which committee, formed alongside the commission, allowed limited Indian consultation through provincial bodies?
options: Central and provincial committees of legislators
questions.explanation: Central and provincial committees of Indian legislators were created to confer with the commission, though most nationalists boycotted them.

questions.q: The intense opposition to the Simon Commission is widely seen as helping push the Congress toward which 1929 demand?
questions.explanation: The exclusion of Indians and the failure of dominion-status hopes fed the Congress's 1929 declaration of Purna Swaraj at Lahore.

questions.q: How many British Members of Parliament chaired by Sir John Simon made up the commission, and from which two parties did they mainly come?
options: Seven, from the Conservative and Labour benches
questions.explanation: The seven-member commission, chaired by the Liberal Sir John Simon, drew its members from across the British parties including Conservatives and Labour.
